response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable generalist engineer with strong distributed systems exposure and a solid testing culture, but he fails to meet the role's most critical hard requirement: 8 years of C++ experience. His C++ usage appears limited to roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project; his primary stack throughout his career has been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Additionally, he has no demonstrated cloud platform architecture leadership at the scale this role demands, and no formal technical lead title with direct mentorship of cloud or DevOps engineers. The mismatch on the minimum qualifications is too significant to overlook.
strengths: Genuine large-scale distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, high-throughput design
Deep and consistent TDD/software-craftsmanship discipline across multiple roles, reinforced by community workshops and open-source testing libraries
ETH Zürich CS degree combined with 15+ years of breadth across backend, infrastructure, embedded, and DevOps work
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient — approximately 6 months at National Instruments versus the mandatory 8-year requirement, and C++ is absent from his listed skills
No production-level cloud platform architecture ownership; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz and Kubernetes at GISCollective are supporting tools, not platform leadership
No formal technical lead role with documented mentorship of cloud or DevOps engineers, which is a preferred qualifier and implied by the seniority level of the position
